Matern Workflow

Overview

ebnm_Matern_generator() builds an ebnm-compatible smoother using a stationary Matern field represented through an INLA/SPDE mesh. The input locations may be one-dimensional or two-dimensional.

The current implementation is identity-link only.

There are now two Matern backends:

  • If pc.penalty = NULL, the fitter uses the exact Gaussian EB backend and optimizes the exact marginal likelihood over (log(range), log(sigma), beta0).
  • If pc.penalty is supplied, the fitter switches to an INLA backend with inla.spde2.pcmatern(). In that case the main log_likelihood is the Step A penalized objective when fix_g = FALSE.

One-Dimensional Example

set.seed(42)

locations_1d <- seq(0, 12, length.out = 120)
truth_1d <- sin(locations_1d / 1.5) + 0.2 * cos(locations_1d)
s_1d <- sample(c(0.15, 0.25, 0.35), length(locations_1d), replace = TRUE)
x_1d <- truth_1d + rnorm(length(locations_1d), sd = s_1d)

fit_fun_1d <- ebnm_Matern_generator(
  locations = locations_1d,
  link = "identity"
)

fit_1d <- fit_fun_1d(x_1d, s_1d)

head(fit_1d$posterior)
#>        mean         var second_moment
#> 1 0.2683555 0.005601723    0.07761642
#> 2 0.2746828 0.005130013    0.08058067
#> 3 0.2937438 0.004445099    0.09073052
#> 4 0.3259030 0.004020085    0.11023286
#> 5 0.3699048 0.003922625    0.14075218
#> 6 0.4232259 0.004013776    0.18313390
fit_1d$fitted_g
#> $theta
#> [1] 1.764491
#> 
#> $sigma
#> [1] 0.7005154
#> 
#> attr(,"class")
#> [1] "Matern"
fit_1d$fitted_beta
#> [1] 0.1035459
fit_1d$backend
#> [1] "exact"
plot(locations_1d, x_1d, pch = 16, cex = 0.45, col = "grey55",
     xlab = "location", ylab = "x",
     main = "Matern smoother in one dimension")
lines(locations_1d, truth_1d, col = "firebrick", lwd = 2, lty = 2)
lines(locations_1d, fit_1d$posterior$mean, col = "navy", lwd = 2)

PC-Prior Backend

Supplying pc.penalty switches the Matern fitter to an INLA backend. This is useful when you want the smoothing hyperparameters to be selected under a PC prior rather than under the exact EB objective.

fit_fun_pc <- ebnm_Matern_generator(
  locations = locations_1d,
  link = "identity",
  pc.penalty = list(
    range = c(1.2, 0.5),
    sigma = c(0.6, 0.5)
  )
)

fit_pc <- fit_fun_pc(x_1d, s_1d)
pc_breakdown <- matern_objective_breakdown(fit_pc)

fit_pc$backend
#> [1] "inla_pc"
fit_pc$fitted_g
#> $theta
#> [1] 1.964932
#> 
#> $sigma
#> [1] 0.8931862
#> 
#> attr(,"class")
#> [1] "Matern"
fit_pc$fitted_beta
#> [1] 0.1033986
fit_pc$log_likelihood
#> 'log Lik.' -15.95084 (df=NULL)
fit_pc$log_likelihood_stepA_mlik_integration
#> [1] -18.58941
pc_breakdown$objective_integrated_flat_plus_prior
#> [1] -15.95084
pc_breakdown$recorded_minus_matched_exact
#> [1] -2.5822e-08
plot(locations_1d, x_1d, pch = 16, cex = 0.45, col = "grey55",
     xlab = "location", ylab = "x",
     main = "Exact EB and PC-prior Matern fits")
lines(locations_1d, truth_1d, col = "firebrick", lwd = 2, lty = 2)
lines(locations_1d, fit_1d$posterior$mean, col = "navy", lwd = 2)
lines(locations_1d, fit_pc$posterior$mean, col = "darkorange3", lwd = 2)
legend(
  "topright",
  legend = c("truth", "exact EB", "INLA PC prior"),
  col = c("firebrick", "navy", "darkorange3"),
  lty = c(2, 1, 1),
  lwd = c(2, 2, 2),
  bty = "n"
)

Fixed Hyperparameters and Fixed Intercept

When fix_g = TRUE, the Matern smoother treats both the smoothing hyperparameters and the intercept as fixed.

set.seed(99)

fit_fixed <- fit_fun_1d(
  x_1d,
  s_1d,
  g_init = Matern(theta = log(1.2), sigma = 0.7),
  fix_g = TRUE,
  beta_fixed = 0.1
)

fit_fixed$fitted_g
#> $theta
#> [1] 0.1823216
#> 
#> $sigma
#> [1] 0.7
#> 
#> attr(,"class")
#> [1] "Matern"
fit_fixed$fitted_beta
#> [1] 0.1
plot(locations_1d, x_1d, pch = 16, cex = 0.45, col = "grey55",
     xlab = "location", ylab = "x",
     main = "Matern smoother with fixed hyperparameters")
lines(locations_1d, truth_1d, col = "firebrick", lwd = 2, lty = 2)
lines(locations_1d, fit_fixed$posterior$mean, col = "darkgreen", lwd = 2)

If you supply both fix_g = TRUE and pc.penalty, the returned log_likelihood is a penalized pseudo-objective:

Step B conditional marginal likelihood + log PC prior on (log(range), log(sigma)).
fit_pc_fixed <- fit_fun_pc(
  x_1d,
  s_1d,
  g_init = Matern(theta = log(1.2), sigma = 0.7),
  fix_g = TRUE,
  beta_fixed = 0.1
)

fit_pc_fixed$log_likelihood
#> 'log Lik.' -38.62205 (df=NULL)
fit_pc_fixed$log_likelihood_stepB
#> [1] -35.84821
fit_pc_fixed$log_likelihood_pc_prior_theta
#> [1] -2.773841

Two-Dimensional Example

For a two-dimensional domain, pass an n x 2 matrix of coordinates.

set.seed(2026)

nx <- 14
ny <- 10
grid <- expand.grid(
  x = seq(0, 1, length.out = nx),
  y = seq(0, 1, length.out = ny)
)
locations_2d <- as.matrix(grid)

truth_2d <- with(
  grid,
  sin(2 * pi * x) * cos(2 * pi * y)
)
s_2d <- rep(0.15, nrow(locations_2d))
x_2d <- truth_2d + rnorm(nrow(locations_2d), sd = s_2d)

fit_fun_2d <- ebnm_Matern_generator(
  locations = locations_2d,
  max.edge = c(0.15, 0.3),
  link = "identity"
)

fit_2d <- fit_fun_2d(x_2d, s_2d)
op <- par(mfrow = c(1, 2), mar = c(4, 4, 3, 5))

z_truth <- matrix(truth_2d, nrow = nx, ncol = ny)
image(
  x = sort(unique(grid$x)),
  y = sort(unique(grid$y)),
  z = z_truth,
  main = "True surface",
  xlab = "x",
  ylab = "y",
  col = hcl.colors(20, "BluYl")
)

z_fit <- matrix(fit_2d$posterior$mean, nrow = nx, ncol = ny)
image(
  x = sort(unique(grid$x)),
  y = sort(unique(grid$y)),
  z = z_fit,
  main = "Posterior mean",
  xlab = "x",
  ylab = "y",
  col = hcl.colors(20, "BluYl")
)


par(op)

Useful Outputs

The Matern fitter returns backend-specific components in addition to the common ebnm-style fields:

names(fit_1d)
#>  [1] "posterior"                "fitted_g"                
#>  [3] "fitted_beta"              "beta_mode"               
#>  [5] "g_init"                   "log_likelihood"          
#>  [7] "log_likelihood_semantics" "posterior_sampler"       
#>  [9] "data"                     "prior_family"            
#> [11] "posterior_spatial_field"  "mesh"                    
#> [13] "inla_result"              "backend"                 
#> [15] "pc_penalty"               "matern_objective_context"
names(fit_pc)
#>  [1] "posterior"                               
#>  [2] "fitted_g"                                
#>  [3] "fitted_beta"                             
#>  [4] "beta_mode"                               
#>  [5] "g_init"                                  
#>  [6] "log_likelihood"                          
#>  [7] "log_likelihood_semantics"                
#>  [8] "posterior_sampler"                       
#>  [9] "data"                                    
#> [10] "prior_family"                            
#> [11] "posterior_spatial_field"                 
#> [12] "mesh"                                    
#> [13] "inla_result"                             
#> [14] "backend"                                 
#> [15] "pc_penalty"                              
#> [16] "log_likelihood_stepA_penalized"          
#> [17] "log_likelihood_stepA_mlik_integration"   
#> [18] "log_likelihood_stepA_mlik_gaussian"      
#> [19] "log_likelihood_stepA_joint_log_posterior"
#> [20] "log_likelihood_exact_at_stepA_mode"      
#> [21] "log_likelihood_stepB"                    
#> [22] "log_likelihood_pc_prior_theta"           
#> [23] "matern_objective_context"
class(fit_1d$mesh)
#> [1] "fm_mesh_1d"   "inla.mesh.1d"
class(fit_1d$inla_result)
#> [1] "NULL"
class(fit_pc$inla_result)
#> [1] "inla"
head(fit_1d$posterior_spatial_field)
#>   ID      mean         sd 0.025quant  0.5quant 0.975quant      mode kld
#> 1  1 0.1648097 0.07484466 0.01811683 0.1648097  0.3115025 0.1648097   0
#> 2  2 0.1711369 0.07162411 0.03075626 0.1711369  0.3115176 0.1711369   0
#> 3  3 0.1901979 0.06667158 0.05952402 0.1901979  0.3208718 0.1901979   0
#> 4  4 0.2223571 0.06340414 0.09808731 0.2223571  0.3466270 0.2223571   0
#> 5  5 0.2663589 0.06263086 0.14360467 0.2663589  0.3891131 0.2663589   0
#> 6  6 0.3196800 0.06335437 0.19550768 0.3196800  0.4438523 0.3196800   0
#>           var
#> 1 0.005601723
#> 2 0.005130013
#> 3 0.004445099
#> 4 0.004020085
#> 5 0.003922625
#> 6 0.004013776

The mesh is still useful for diagnostics and visualization. The posterior_spatial_field component contains a latent-field summary for the mesh nodes.

  • In exact mode, inla_result is NULL, posterior_sampler is available, and the main log_likelihood is the exact EB marginal likelihood.
  • In pc.penalty mode, inla_result stores the INLA fit object, the main log_likelihood is backend-dependent, and posterior_sampler is currently a compatibility stub that returns NA.
